sections.content: Radar reflectors are passive devices that enhance a vessel's visibility on other ships' radar screens. Small vessels made of fiberglass or wood produce very weak radar echoes, making them nearly invisible to approaching ships. A properly mounted radar reflector significantly increases the radar cross-section.

The most common types are the octahedral (corner) reflector and the lens-type reflector. Octahedral reflectors consist of metal plates arranged to reflect radar signals back to their source. They should be mounted as high as possible, at least 4 metres above the waterline, in the "rain-catching" position for maximum effectiveness.

sections.title: SOLAS Requirement
sections.content: SOLAS requires all vessels under 150 GT to carry a radar reflector or equivalent means to make them detectable by radar. Lifeboats and liferafts should also be equipped with radar reflectors.

sections.prompt: What is the purpose of a radar reflector?

options.text: To enhance radar visibility of small vessels
To detect other vessels' radar emissions
To send distress signals via radar

sections.explanation: Radar reflectors are passive devices that enhance a vessel's radar cross-section, making it more visible on other ships' radar screens. This is critical for small vessels that would otherwise be difficult to detect.
